_meta.description: Per-category audience multipliers applied to ZIP-level Census ACS baselines. Values >1.0 over-index, <1.0 under-index. Hand-curated v1 estimates derived from standard DMA segmentation; refine with measured data when available.

Bar/Restaurant.label: Bar / Restaurant
Bar/Restaurant.description: Patrons trend young-to-middle adult with disposable income and a social/discretionary mindset.

Coffee/Donut/Bagel Shop.label: Coffee / Bakery
Coffee/Donut/Bagel Shop.description: Heavy on commuters, students, and white-collar professionals stopping in on routine.

Barbershop/Salon.label: Barber / Salon
Barbershop/Salon.description: Long captive dwell. Salons skew female 25-54; barbershops skew male 18-44.

Health & Fitness.label: Gym / Fitness
Health & Fitness.description: Health-conscious adults, often with above-median income and education.

Medical.label: Medical Office
Medical.description: Broadest demographic of any venue — anyone with a health need. Slight skew older for primary care/specialist offices.

Retail.label: Retail
Retail.description: Mainstream shoppers — close to general-population mix with a slight female skew at boutique categories.

Gas/ Grocery.label: Gas / Grocery
Gas/ Grocery.description: Highest-frequency stop in the network. Reaches close to the entire ZIP demographic.

Liquor/Wine/Beer Store.label: Liquor / Wine / Beer
Liquor/Wine/Beer Store.description: Strictly 21+. Skews male, middle-income, evening visit.

Family Rec & Entertainment.label: Family Rec / Entertainment
Family Rec & Entertainment.description: Parents with children plus social groups. Strong household-with-kids skew.

Professional Services.label: Professional Services
Professional Services.description: Office visitors and clients of accountants, lawyers, advisors. Skews college-educated and higher-income.

Travel & Tourism.label: Travel & Tourism
Travel & Tourism.description: Mix of locals and visitors. Visitor demographic varies by event/season; treated as ZIP baseline with slight income lift.

Education.label: Education
Education.description: College towns: skews 18-24 heavily, plus parents at family-facing K-12 venues.

Non Profit, Community, Government.label: Community / Civic
Non Profit, Community, Government.description: Cross-section of the community by design. Treated near ZIP baseline.

Auto Shop/Auto Dealer/Oil Change.label: Auto Service / Dealer
Auto Shop/Auto Dealer/Oil Change.description: Vehicle owners — adults 25-64, mix of household incomes, often making spend decisions.

Other.label: Other
Other.description: Mixed/uncategorized venues. Treated as ZIP baseline with no skew applied.
